name: eval-1-lock-working-workflow
context_setup: Previous turns: user walked Claude through a specific process for generating a client-ready proposal from a Notion scoping doc — read the doc, extract the problem/approach/timeline sections, apply the Summit branding template, produce a PDF with cover page + scope + pricing. It worked well across two different client examples.
query: Lock this workflow so I can reuse it.
expected_behavior: Phase 1 (Identify What Worked): reviews the chat history and extracts the pattern — input (Notion scoping doc URL or text), sequence (extract sections, apply template, generate PDF), output (client-ready PDF)

Phase 2 (Templatize): identifies what changes each time (client name, specific problem/approach/timeline content, pricing) and parameterizes those — produces a template with placeholders like {{client_name}}, {{scoping_doc}}, {{pricing_tier}}
Identifies the trigger conditions: when the user says 'generate a proposal' or references a Notion scoping doc and client preparation context

Phase 3 (Output the Locked Workflow): produces a structured workflow definition with name, trigger, inputs, steps, outputs, and any rules (e.g., 'always use Summit template v2, never put pricing on cover page')
Phase 4 (Offer Next Steps): offers to chain to prompt-to-skill to make it permanent — 'Want me to install this as a permanent skill so it activates automatically when you mention a proposal?'
Does NOT auto-chain to prompt-to-skill without asking
failure_modes_to_check: Saves the exact ad-hoc session without parameterizing — produces a workflow that only works for the one client already used
Forgets the trigger conditions, so the workflow has no activation path
Skips the offer to install permanently via prompt-to-skill
